what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the file-discovered GenerationPolicyRegistry: it verifies seed-policy discovery, dynamic drop-in/removal of policy files, fail-loud validation of policy shape, rep_penalty ladder semantics, absence of .resolve calls in the runtime module, and reflection of every policy in generation_policies/AGENTS.md.
observations: Bootstraps sys.path and chdir to the project root at import time.
Uses a module-level PASS counter incremented by a custom check helper.
Discovers policies from the generation_policies directory and asserts seed ids capture_default and prose_default are present.
Writes a temporary policy file into generation_policies, rediscovers, then deletes it to test dynamic registration and un-registration.
Validates malformed policies via a raises helper that expects ValueError or TypeError.
Asserts specific ladder values: capture_default [1.1, 1.2] and prose_default [1.1].
Scans runtime/generation_policies.py source for any .resolve( call after stripping line comments.
Verifies every discovered policy id and the registry class name appear in generation_policies/AGENTS.md.
standouts: Tests dynamic discovery by actually writing and removing a file in the source tree during execution.
Embeds a specific ladder semantics test where exhaustion returns None as a fail-loud degenerate-loop signal.
Includes a static-source audit for a forbidden .resolve( token as a floor-breach guard.
Treats AGENTS.md parity as an acceptance criterion.
conventions: Heavy module docstring with NEWMOD/O2/P1 tags and numbered mechanistic claims.
Manual sys.path insertion and os.chdir before imports, marked with noqa E402.
Global mutable PASS counter for test accounting.
Small helper functions check and raises for assertion style.
Hardcoded seed ids and expected ladder values tied to seed policy files.
concerns: Side effects at import time: sys.path mutation and cwd change.
Writes and removes a file inside the production source tree, which can race or leave artifacts on interruption.
raises catches only ValueError/TypeError, so other exceptions leak as test failures rather than expected validation failures.
Comment-stripping regex for the .resolve( audit can mangle string literals containing #.
Global PASS state and print-based reporting are not isolated per test.
notes: A behavioral contract test that treats the generation-policy registry as a file-discovered, validated, documented subsystem.
questions: What is the exact difference between discover and rediscover on GenerationPolicyRegistry?
How is generation_policies/AGENTS.md generated and kept in sync outside this test?
What is the C9.2 floor token and COG_SOURCES enrollment referenced in the no-.resolve check?
Which test runner is intended for this script, given its global PASS counter and print output?
Are the seed policy files capture_default.py and prose_default.py versioned alongside this test?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it is an acceptance test proving file-discovered registry behavior, dynamic registration, fail-loud validation, ladder-as-data semantics, no .resolve token, and drift-home parity.
apparent_themes: Documentation parity as a runtime acceptance gate
Avoidance of specific floor-breach method-call patterns
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce that generation policies are not hardcoded but discovered from policy files, that malformed policies raise immediately, that ladder escalation is encoded as data, that the runtime module avoids a particular .resolve call pattern, and that the registry stays reflected in its AGENTS.md drift home.
novelty: Combines dynamic file-drop acceptance testing with a static source-code token prohibition check and an explicit documentation-parity assertion.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that documents and guards the generation-policies registry contract: dynamic file discovery, strict validation rules, rep_penalty ladder semantics, a source-code token prohibition, and documentation parity.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for a file-discovered generation-policy registry that validates dynamic registration, fail-loud policy validation, rep_penalty ladder semantics, absence of .resolve calls, and AGENTS.md drift-home parity.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by mechanism that generation policies are data-driven, dynamically discovered, strictly validated, and kept in sync with their documentation.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok line for each passing check.
symbols.description: Takes a descriptive label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL message on failure, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, and prints a success line. It is the central accounting mechanism for the acceptance test.

symbols.does: Helper that expects a callable to raise ValueError or TypeError and records the outcome via check.
symbols.description: Wraps a zero-argument callable, treats ValueError or TypeError as the expected fail-loud behavior, and delegates to check to record success or failure. It is used to assert validation failures for malformed generation policies.
